prompt: Extract requirements from the OpenTelemetry architecture specification at https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/otel/overview/ — give me a full requirements spec. What is the system designed to do, what are its goals for implementors, what assumptions does it make, and what are the non-negotiable design constraints?

assertions.text: Output contains all four sections: Mission, Goals, Premises, and Constraints as distinct labeled sections
Mission captures vendor-neutral observability portability as the core purpose (not just 'collect telemetry')
Goals mention at least two of the three signal types: traces, metrics, and logs
Constraints or Premises captures the API/SDK separation — that the API must be stable and separately versionable from the SDK
Premises include that the specification is language-agnostic (implemented across many languages)
The spec correctly treats this as a design/architecture document rather than a user-facing product — requirements reflect implementor constraints, not end-user features

Constraints are explicitly split into Hard constraints (violation = rejection / spec non-compliance) and Soft constraints (violation = penalty / degraded quality) as separate subsections or clearly labeled categories
Premises are presented in a table format that includes a 'Risk if false' or 'Consequence if false' column assessing what breaks if each assumption is wrong
